---
title: "🗓️ A Gentle Introduction to R"
---

In preparation for the module you should begin completing the introductory R workshop. This workshop is designed for those who have no prior knowledge in R. Hence, please feel free to skip the parts if you feel confident about the introductory knowledge. 

## 👨‍🏫 Workshop Slides

Either click on the slide area below or click [here](Rworkshop.qmd) to view it in fullscreen. Use your keypad to navigate the slides.

::: {.container style="position: relative;overflow: hidden;width: 100%;min-height:600px;"}
<iframe src="Rworkshop.html" style="position=absolute;top: 0;left: 0;bottom: 0;right: 0;width: 100%;min-height:600px;">

</iframe>
:::

🎥 Looking for the workshop recording? You can only find those on Moodle.

## ✍️ Lab Materials

🚧 Come back after the lecture to read the practicals for the week 🚧

## 📚 Required Reading

-   [Edelman, A., Wolff, T., Montagne, D. & Bail, C. A. (2020). Computational Social Science and Sociology, Annual Review of Sociology.](https://www.annualreviews.org/doi/10.1146/annurev-soc-121919-054621)

-   [Bryan, J. (2018), Happy Git and GitHub for the useR, GitHub.](https://happygitwithr.com/)
